data.body:
PLAN REVIEW · CONTAINER · [G3] Child coverage — pairing (parent 6f2d ⟶ child b4af WS-EVAL-EXISTING) — VERDICT: CLEAN (no findings).

Scope note: the parent epic's AUTHORITATIVE residual scope (per the latest SCOPE-DECISION comment) narrowed to WS-DOC + WS-EVAL-EXISTING (WS-VISUAL-EVAL split off into its own epic; WS-MIGRATE/WS-PROMPTS/WS-CLEANUP/WS-VISUAL/WS-VALIDATE/WS-INTENT-ALIGN all marked DONE via closed sibling epics 5766/72d5/e6d9/681b). The 2-child roster is WS-DOC (4024) + WS-EVAL-EXISTING (b4af). This pairing reviews b4af against the WS-EVAL-EXISTING lane.

4-bucket audit of the parent's WS-EVAL-EXISTING acceptance/success criteria vs this child:
- Eval coverage for verify_completion / review_ticket(ticket-quality) / scan_spec(spec-alignment) — FULLY (Phase-1 authors the 3 specs; Phase-2 finishes the shared run machinery so they actually gate).
- Eval coverage for review_code (code-quality) — FULLY (covered-by-definition / ANTI-FP): the parent body names FOUR migrated reviewers (verify_completion + review_ticket + review_code + scan_spec), but the child's title/Phase-1 names only THREE. The fourth (review_code) is NOT absent — it is the pre-existing code-quality.eval.yaml, which the child explicitly brings under the gate via "all 7 specs" in AC2 (scorer registry maps every name used by all 7), AC3 (run_eval() live for all specs), and AC4 (eval-discipline gates all 7 on every PR). 7 = 4 existing (code-quality + plan-review finder/verifier/isf) + 3 new. Reconciles cleanly; not an uncovered-criterion finding.
- recall / false-accept / false-fire metrics — FULLY (structural): carried by AC1 non-empty gold_set + AC4 offline "dataset balance" assertion + Phase-2.4 fixture corpus & label adjudication, matching the established convention in the existing plan-review eval specs and parity.py.
- Drawn from the three corpuses (DSO read-only + snap-oakhart-manual + rebar tickets) — FULLY (Phase-2.4 fixture corpus + per-reviewer dataset schema; provenance is a property of that deliverable).
- "parallel-run-and-diff old vs new, made into a standing eval" — FULLY (the child's stated purpose: regression-gated, "not a blind cutover").
- Phase 1 + Phase 2 ("make evals actually run" — run_eval() stub, inert scorer names, placeholder eval-live step) — FULLY (Phase-2.1–2.5).

No parent criterion in this child's lane is left uncovered; the lone apparent gap (review_code) survives the roster/codebase cross-check as covered-by-definition. Empty findings list for G3 on this pairing.
